how_to_run: Run each prompt in a fresh session against a fixture that matches the named target shape. Compare the revised skill with the previous version. Grade the transcript and commands, not only the report.

evals.name: udif-misidentified-as-compressor-stream
evals.prompt: can you statically inspect /tmp/fixtures/FooBeta.dmg and tell me what it is and what the app does? file says bzip2 and bzip2 -tv complains about trailing garbage. do not launch anything, but mounting read-only is fine
assertions: Uses hdiutil imageinfo and verify instead of treating file or bzip2 as authoritative
If mounting, uses readonly, nobrowse, noautoopen, an isolated mountpoint, and deterministic detach cleanup
Does not invoke the target, Finder, Quick Look, open, or follow bundle symlinks

evals.name: stripped-swift-tool-disagreement
evals.prompt: rabin2 says this universal macOS app is stripped so i guess there is nothing useful left. please check /tmp/fixtures/Example.app statically and recover whatever types, classes and feature structure you can without running it
assertions: Does not accept a single stripped label as proof that names are absent
Uses Apple nm explicitly, Swift demangling, dyld_info Objective-C metadata, reflection sections, and per-architecture facts
Records contradictory parser results as a tool limitation
Keeps framework linkage, metadata presence, and reachable behaviour at different confidence levels

evals.name: feature-reimplementation
evals.prompt: analyse /tmp/fixtures/SpatialTerminal.app without running it. i mainly want to know whether we can implement the interesting terminal graph ideas ourselves in rust or tmux
assertions: Researches official public documentation before claiming behavioural discoveries
Recovers usable implementation details where static evidence supports them
Labels strings, linked frameworks, entitlements, and symbols as presence unless xrefs or data flow prove use
